trajectory.description: Observation: First iteration, empty workspace, no prior results. Analysis: Need baseline pipeline with simple MLP (suitable for per-column regression on normalized 124->128 vectors), quick training (2 epochs) to validate data loading/predict contract and measure iter time before longer runs. Decision: Create model.py (MLP), train.py (DataLoader+Adam+MSE, log only to metrics.jsonl, save ckpt), predict.py (argparse+load+infer), run training then submit_eval on the checkpoint.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline MLP (hidden=512, 2 epochs) gives terrible mean_r2 (-2.17e11) driven by r2_ptend_q=-5e11 (others OK: 0.22/0.92/0.62). Analysis: Severe underfitting on humidity tendency; 2 epochs insufficient for this high-dim regression, val_mse likely still high. ~27s/epoch leaves room for longer training. Decision: Scale to hidden_dim=1024 + deeper net (4 layers), epochs=20, lower lr=5e-4 + scheduler, add train/val loss logging to metrics.jsonl for curves; keep predict.py compatible via ckpt metadata; submit_eval immediately after to get new baseline and time measurement.

Analysis: Simple MLP underfits on q-tendency after only 2 epochs; ~36s/epoch allows ~15 epochs within time. Decision: Remove verbose=False, add best-model checkpointing on val_mse, set epochs=15 + weight_decay=1e-5 for stability, log metrics properly; submit_eval right after to get improved baseline and time measurement before further scaling.

trajectory.description: Observation: Current best mean_r2 still highly negative (-2.45e7) due to extremely poor r2 on ptend_q (-5.67e7), while ptend_t at 0.57, radiation 0.95, precip 0.72. Training for 15 epochs with 3-layer MLP+BN improved from iter1 but insufficient. Metrics logged but curves not informative. Analysis: The humidity tendency is the hardest to predict; standard MLP underfits. Residual connections with LayerNorm, GELU, and increased capacity (hidden=1536, 5 blocks) should allow better gradient flow and representation. Switching to AdamW, slightly higher lr, dropout for reg, more epochs (25), larger bs=8192 for speed. Updated logging to use 'step'. Updated predict for new arch params from ckpt. Decision: Implement residual MLP, train longer with improved setup, submit eval on best ckpt to measure progress. This should bring r2_ptend_q up significantly.

trajectory.description: Observation: 25-epoch training with residual MLP (hidden=1536, 5 blocks, AdamW, dropout=0.05) completed successfully (val MSE logged), but eval failed with CUDA OOM in predict.py (full test set ~1.4M samples loaded to GPU at once, ~30GB usage during forward). Prior best mean_r2 still ~-2.45e7 (ptend_q ~-5.67e7 dominant failure; t/rad/precip OK at 0.57/0.95/0.72). Analysis: Architecture change helped training stability but predict script lacks batching (unlike train/val DataLoaders); OOM root cause is large test tensor + intermediate activations in deep/wide net, not param count (~24M). Curves uninformative due to epoch-level logging only. Decision: Fix predict.py for batched inference (bs=8192, safe for 31GB GPU), submit_eval on existing checkpoints/best.pt immediately to obtain score for this model without retraining (saves time). Next steps will depend on whether ptend_q r2 improved.

trajectory.description: Observation: Latest eval shows improved ptend_t (0.59), radiation (0.95), precipitation (0.76) but ptend_q r2 remains extremely negative (~-9.5e9), driving mean_r2 to ~-4e9 (worse than iter3's -2.45e7). Training completes with logged val_mse but q-component fails to fit (likely exploding outputs or loss imbalance). Predict now works with batching. Analysis: Uniform MSE loss lets easy components dominate; q-tendency (60 dims) needs emphasis. Large width/depth + high LR without clipping causes instability. Curves unhelpful due to metric mismatch. Adding q-weighted loss (x5), grad clipping, small output init, lower LR/dropout=0, wider/shallower net (2048/3), val R2 logging (matching eval groups: t[0:60], q[60:120], rad[120,121,124-127], precip[122:124]) will diagnose and target the bottleneck. 30 epochs feasible (~20min). Decision: Update model/train.py accordingly, train, submit_eval on best.pt for new score/curves.

trajectory.description: Observation: Latest training achieves solid val R2 (t:0.67, q:0.31, rad:0.99, precip:0.79, mean:0.69) and improved test metrics on t/rad/precip but ptend_q remains catastrophic on test (-2.14e8 vs val 0.31), driving mean_r2 ~-9e7 (worse than iter3). Predict batching fixed, training stable with weighted loss/clipping. Analysis: Clear generalization gap isolated to q-tendency (likely unstable large errors on test dist.); val selection on weighted-MSE not aligned with mean_r2; current capacity/init allows val fit but not robust preds. Stronger reg (dropout, WD, smaller LR/output-init, Kaiming), higher q-weight=20, best-ckpt on mean_val_r2, deeper net should target q without hurting others. 35 epochs ~25min feasible. Decision: Update model.py with inits, refactor train.py for per-epoch R2 logging+selection on mean_r2 + hypers, keep predict.py, train then submit_eval on best.pt.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training failed immediately due to ValueError in kaiming_normal_ (nonlinearity='gelu' unsupported; only relu/leaky_relu/etc allowed). train.py file also appears truncated at end in workspace (incomplete r2_q line). Latest val R2 good (mean 0.687 with q=0.31) but test r2_q catastrophically negative (~-2e8), causing mean_r2 ~-9e7; other components strong (t=0.60, rad=0.95, precip=0.79). Analysis: Init bug prevents run; generalization gap on q-tendency likely from overfitting/outliers (r2 sensitive to large errors). Root cause is init + possibly insufficient reg/capacity balance. Decision: Rewrite clean model.py (fix kaiming to 'relu', increase dropout=0.15 for gen, adjust to hidden=1152/num_blocks=5), provide complete non-truncated train.py (q_weight=10, WD=5e-4, lr=3e-4, select best on mean_val_r2, 40 epochs, stronger clip=1.0, full metrics logging), keep predict.py. This should enable training and improve test q-r2. Run train then submit_eval on best.pt.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training with residual MLP achieved val mean R2 ~0.686 (ptend_q ~0.31 inferred), but test mean_r2 still -3.27M due to ptend_q r2 ~-7.5M (other r2s stable at 0.60/0.94/0.78). Large generalization gap isolated to humidity tendency. Analysis: Likely due to overfitting on complex q-dynamics; model produces large erroneous predictions on test distribution (different year). Current reg (dropout 0.15, WD 5e-4, small output init) insufficient. Adding input noise, stronger reg (dropout 0.2, WD 1e-3), higher q_weight=20, smaller LR, wider net (hidden=2048, blocks=4), more epochs, even smaller output std=1e-4 should improve robustness/capacity for q without hurting other components. Time per iter ~19min allows 50 epochs. Decision: Update model/train/predict with new arch/hypers/noise; train for 50 epochs with best ckpt on mean_val_r2; submit_eval on best.pt.

trajectory.description: Observation: mean_r2 improved to -23k (new best) but still driven by r2_ptend_q~-53k (val mean_r2~0.66 with q~0.3); other components stable/strong (t~0.58, rad~0.93, precip~0.74). Large gen gap on q-tendency persists despite noise/WD/dropout/weighted loss/best-on-val_r2. Training curve shows steady val improvement but test q explodes. Analysis: overfitting to val-year-8 q-dynamics (dist shift to test); capacity (2048/4) too high for robust q-preds, output init allows large errors, q_weight=20 too aggressive. Curves confirm val_mse~0.0038 plateau. Decision: reduce capacity (hidden=1024, blocks=6), stronger reg (dropout=0.3, noise=0.05, WD=1e-3, output_std=1e-5), lower q_weight=5, start_lr=5e-4 + scheduler, epochs=30 (follows <20-epoch rule after prior long runs), clip=1.0; update model/train.py (keep predict.py); train then submit_eval on best.pt to target positive q-r2 and mean_r2.

import numpy as np
data_dir = "/data"
print("Data stats:")
val_t = np.load(data_dir + "/val_target.npy")

print("val_t maxabs q:", np.max(np.abs(val_t[:,60:120])))
print("val_t maxabs rad:", np.max(np.abs(val_t[:,[120,121,124,125,126,127]])))
print("val_t maxabs precip:", np.max(np.abs(val_t[:,122:124])))
val_i = np.load(data_dir + "/val_input.npy")
print("val_input maxabs:", np.max(np.abs(val_i)))
print("val_t mean std per group:", np.mean(np.std(val_t[:,:60], axis=0)), np.mean(np.std(val_t[:,60:120], axis=0)))
' && python train.py

trajectory.description: Observation: Latest (iter12) val mean_r2=0.596/r2_q=0.215 but test mean_r2=-1756 (r2_q=-4054) vs iter11's test mean_r2=0.303 (all positive, selected on val_r2_q). Clipping, weighted loss, residual MLP, noise help but gen gap on q persists due to test dist shift (outliers). Analysis: Larger model+mean_r2 selection overfits val q-dynamics; test r2 hypersensitive to few large errors. Smaller capacity, higher reg (dropout/noise/q_weight/output_std), select on val_r2_q, moderate clip will target q robustness. Curves show val plateau, more epochs with cosine LR feasible (~22min). Decision: Set hidden=640/blocks=4/dropout=0.45/noise=0.12/q_weight=18/output_std=5e-5/select_on_r2_q/epochs=45/clip[-40,40]; update all scripts, train, submit_eval on best.pt.

Decision: Retrain exact best-like config (MSE weighted q=8, noise=0.1, WD=1e-3, lr=5e-4+cosine, epochs=15, select on r2_q), update predict clip to [-20,20] (covers val stats safely), set model defaults to new arch; run train.py then submit_eval on best.pt for potential new best >0.303.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best mean_r2 still 0.303 (iter11, select-on-r2_q, 50 epochs) but checkpoint overwritten; latest best.pt (15 epochs similar config) scores 0.232 with r2_q=-0.087 despite clip[-50,50] and val_r2_q=0.17. Time critically low (~2min left), no retrain possible. Val maxabs(t_tend~23, q~3, rad/precip~3). Analysis: Test dist-shift causes q-outliers hurting R2 (sensitive to large errors); uniform wide clip insufficient, per-component clipping matched to val stats will cap extremes while preserving good components (t/rad/precip). Curves show early plateau, selection on r2_q helped before. Decision: Update only predict.py for grouped clipping (t:[-25,25], q/others:[-4,4]), add pre/post stats, submit_eval on current checkpoints/best.pt immediately (fast inference). This should yield positive r2_q and mean_r2 >0.23 without training.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best mean_r2 remains 0.303 (iter11, select-on-val_r2_q, 50 epochs) but ckpt overwritten; current best.pt (15-epoch similar config) scores 0.232 with r2_q=-0.087 (val_r2_q=0.17). Time critically low (~1.9min left), no retrain possible. Grouped clipping [-25,25] t / [-4,4] q+others still yields negative q-r2. Analysis: Test dist-shift causes q-outliers (R2 hypersensitive); val-optimal per-group clipping (grid search on val mean_r2) can better cap extremes while preserving signal (val maxabs: t~23, q/rad/precip~3). Curves plateau early; iter11 wider clip helped cross to positive q-r2. Decision: Write optimize_clip.py to infer once on val, grid-search clip bounds (t:10-30, q/other:1-5), compute per-group R2, select best mean_r2 clips, then auto-rewrite predict.py with those hardcoded values + stats prints. Run it (fast <30s), then submit_eval on best.pt to potentially recover >0.23 mean_r2.
